meta.name: SCS-SF Self-Compassion Scale Short Form
meta.description: The SCS-SF is a 12-item brief measure of self-compassion, assessing six components: Self-Kindness, Self-Judgment, Common Humanity, Isolation, Mindfulness, and Over-Identification.
meta.author: Raes et al. (2011)
meta.reference: Raes F, Pommier E, Neff KD, Van Gucht D. Construction and factorial validation of a short form of the Self-Compassion Scale. Clin Psychol Psychother. 2011;18(3):250-255.
meta.instruction: Please read each statement carefully and indicate how often you behave in the stated manner.

questions.text: When I fail at something important to me I become consumed by feelings of inadequacy
I try to be understanding and patient towards those aspects of my personality I don't like
When something painful happens I tend to blow the incident out of proportion
When I'm feeling down, I tend to feel like most other people are probably happier than I am
I try to see my failings as part of the human condition
When I'm going through a very hard time, I give myself the caring and tenderness I need
When something upsets me I try to keep my emotions in balance
When I fail at something that's important to me, I tend to feel alone in my failure
When I'm feeling down I try to approach my feelings with curiosity and openness
When I feel inadequate in some way, I try to remind myself that feelings of inadequacy are shared by most people
I'm disapproving and judgmental about my own flaws and inadequacies
I'm intolerant and impatient towards those aspects of my personality I don't like

ranges.label: Low Self-Compassion
ranges.description: Your self-compassion is quite low, suggesting a tendency toward self-criticism, isolation during difficult times, and over-identification with negative thoughts. Low self-compassion is associated with increased vulnerability to depression, anxiety, and burnout.
suggestions: Practice self-compassion exercises from Kristin Neff’s work — try the self-compassion break meditation
Challenge your inner critic by asking ‘What would I say to a close friend in this situation?’
Explore Compassion-Focused Therapy (CFT) or Mindful Self-Compassion (MSC) programs
Practice self-kindness through physical gestures like placing a hand on your heart during difficult moments
Consider working with a therapist trained in self-compassion approaches

ranges.label: Moderate Self-Compassion
ranges.description: Your self-compassion is at a moderate level. You show some ability to treat yourself kindly during difficulties but may still default to self-criticism under stress. Strengthening self-compassion skills can improve emotional resilience and well-being.
suggestions: Practice loving-kindness meditation to cultivate warmth toward yourself and others
Notice self-critical thoughts and gently reframe them with self-compassion
Remind yourself that suffering and imperfection are shared human experiences
Try journaling about difficult experiences from a compassionate perspective

ranges.label: Above Average Self-Compassion
ranges.description: Your self-compassion is above average, indicating a healthy balance of self-kindness, common humanity, and mindful awareness during difficult times. This capacity helps buffer against stress and supports psychological flexibility.
suggestions: Maintain your self-compassion practices during challenging periods
Share self-compassion techniques with friends, family, or colleagues who might benefit
Continue developing mindfulness skills to support present-moment awareness

ranges.label: High Self-Compassion
ranges.description: Your self-compassion is very strong. You consistently treat yourself with kindness, recognize your struggles as part of the shared human experience, and maintain balanced awareness of your emotions. This is a significant protective factor for mental health.
suggestions: Continue your self-compassion practices to sustain this valuable inner resource
Consider mentoring or teaching others about self-compassion
Use your strong self-compassion foundation to take on new challenges and growth opportunities
